untranslatable_reason: The signal requires explicit range boundaries (range_boundaries, lower_end_definition, upper_end_definition) which are marked AUTHOR_SILENT in the original specification. Without knowing how the author defines the range extremes, precise translation is impossible. Used Donchian channels as a reasonable proxy for range boundaries, but this may not match the author's intended logic.
translation_notes: Signal is context-dependent (long at lower boundary, short at upper boundary). Since exact range definitions are not provided by the author, used dc_upper and dc_lower (20-period Donchian channels) as a standard proxy for range-bound market extremes. Entry long triggers when price reaches lower end; entry short when reaching upper end. Exits occur at opposite boundaries (reversal condition). This assumes a mean-reversion strategy within a defined range.
